criteria.text: User can submit the form with valid input and see a success message.
adversarial_cases: Submit valid input twice in rapid succession (under 200ms apart); only one record should be created server-side and the UI should show success exactly once.

observations.content: Loaded form. Filled name='Alice', email='alice@example.com'. Clicked Submit.
Re-filled valid input. Clicked Submit twice within ~100ms.

evidence: Submit valid input twice in rapid succession (under 200ms apart); only one record should be created server-side and the UI should show success exactly once.
No console errors observed during submit flow
User can submit the form with valid input and see a success message.
Plan does not include rate-limiting, analytics, or user-tracking integrations.

grounding.statement: Immediate success confirmation closes the loop on the user's core 'did it save?' anxiety.
grounding.decision_test: Success affordance visible within 250ms of the 201 response — met (toast at +1420ms vs request at +1380ms).

criteria.text: Form validation rejects empty required fields with an inline error message.

evidence: (Verify pass only exercised valid-input submission; empty-required-field path is not observable.)
Form validation rejects empty required fields with an inline error message.
correctness.notes: Verify pass only exercised valid-input submission; empty-required-field path is not observable from this output.

evidence: Form validation rejects empty required fields with an inline error message.
regression.notes: Adjacent regression behavior cannot be assessed without the empty-field path being exercised.

evidence: Form validation rejects empty required fields with an inline error message.
scope-creep.notes: Validation surfaces not exercised; cannot assess whether implementation added out-of-scope validation behavior.

not_tested.item: Real network (used dev server; no production CDN, no real latency)
>1 device class / OS version (only one simulator tested)

open_questions.question: The success toast auto-dismisses at 2s — keep 2s, or hold until the next interaction?
open_questions.rationale: 2s matches the design-language default, but submit is a trust-sensitive moment where a lingering confirmation may reassure more.
open_questions.recommended_default: Keep 2s (design-language default) unless a usability signal says otherwise.
open_questions.user_need_lens: First-time user verifying their data actually saved.

open_questions.question: Should empty-field validation copy be tone-matched to the brand voice guide, or stay terse?
open_questions.rationale: Out of this iteration's scope; flagged so it is not lost.
open_questions.recommended_default: Defer to a dedicated copy pass.
